# ansible-win

> Generic Windows 11 workstation bootstrap and onboarding automation.
> PowerShell scripts for OpenSSH, firewall, Chocolatey, UV, and
> Windows-MCP setup, plus an Ansible collection scaffold for day-2 drift
> management.

## Overview

- Name: ansible-win
- Purpose: Windows 11 workstation bootstrap (PowerShell admin scripts) and Ansible collection scaffold for day-2 configuration drift management.
- Language(s): PowerShell, YAML (Ansible)
- License: MIT
- Authoritative README: <https://github.com/nfsarch33/ansible-win#readme>
- Source of truth branch: `main`

## Quick Start

- Run PowerShell as Administrator on a Windows 11 target:
  ```powershell
  Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope Process -ExecutionPolicy Bypass
  .\install-all.ps1
  ```
- Minimum runtime prerequisites: Windows 11, PowerShell 5.1+

## Key Concepts

- `ansible/` — Ansible collection scaffold for Windows day-2 drift management
- `docs/repo-boundaries.md` — ownership rules for this repo
- Root `*.ps1` — legacy workstation bootstrap scripts (OpenSSH, firewall, Chocolatey, UV, Windows-MCP)
- Boundary: generic bootstrap only; operator-specific DevOps/SysAdmin scripts live elsewhere

## Runnable Examples

- Full bootstrap: `.\install-all.ps1` (as Administrator)

## Operational Notes

- Issue tracker: <https://github.com/nfsarch33/ansible-win/issues>
- Do not commit real secrets, fleet hostnames, vault names, or operator-specific paths; use generic placeholders

*This file follows the emerging `llms.txt` convention adopted by cloudwego/eino,
Anthropic tooling, and others.*
